title: aegilops × loretii
intro: Aegilops × loretii, also known as Loret's goatgrass or Loret's spelt, is a hybrid of Aegilops seARSII and Aegilops cylindrica, and is a type of wild grass native to Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Aegilops × loretii has been traditionally used to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and improve immune function, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Aegilops × loretii can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
